name: Rep. Gerry Connolly
tweet: 💵 The Paycheck Fairness Act.  On average, Black women make 63 cents on the dollar a white man makes. For Native women, it’s 60 cents and for Latinas, it’s 55 cents. The Paycheck Fairness Act guarantees that women across the workforce are finally paid their fair share.

name: Rep. Gerry Connolly
tweet: 🚫 Gun Control.  The House has passed bills to expand background checks on all gun purchases and to close the Charleston loophole, which carelessly restricts the period of time the FBI has to conduct a background check to three days — allowing many buyers to evade them.

name: Rep. Gerry Connolly
tweet: 👩‍🏭 The PRO Act.  Puts power back into the hands of workers and restores fairness to the economy by strengthening the federal laws that protect workers’ right to join a union — allowing them to organize for better pay, benefits, and working conditions.

name: Rep. Gerry Connolly
tweet: 🏳️‍🌈 The Equality Act.  Guarantees full civil rights to LGBTQ+ individuals so no person can be discriminated against — in employment, housing, health care, education, and more — on the basis of their sexual preferences or gender identity.

name: Rep. Gerry Connolly
tweet: 🦋 The Dream and Promise Act.  Protects Dreamers from deportation and establishes a pathway to citizenship for millions of people who came to America at an early age.

name: Rep. Gerry Connolly
tweet: ⚖️ The George Floyd Justice in Policing Act.  Reimagines American policing by banning chokeholds, establishing a police misconduct registry,  reforming qualified immunity, ending the militarization of law enforcement, and investing money into Black and brown communities.

name: Rep. Gerry Connolly
tweet: 🗳️ H.R. 1, the For the People Act.   Takes on voter suppression efforts, gets big money OUT of politics,  returns the power in our democracy to the people, protects and expands voting rights to ensure that every voice is heard and every vote is counted.

name: Rep. Gerry Connolly
tweet: Congress must act. We have legislation ready now, and we have more on the way.  This is a rare moment of stark right and wrong. I urge @senatemajldr McConnell, and all my Republican colleagues, to think long and hard before choosing a side.

name: Rep. Gerry Connolly
tweet: 8. @RepRoKhanna's PEACE Act, which would change the use of force standard for federal officers to require that force must be necessary, as a last resort, to prevent imminent death or serious bodily injury.

name: Rep. Gerry Connolly
tweet: 7. @RepJasonCrow's Law Enforcement Trust and Integrity Act, which provides incentives for local police departments to voluntarily adopt performance-based standards to minimize incidents of misconduct, improve operations and enhance community accountability.

name: Rep. Gerry Connolly
tweet: 6. @RepGwenMoore's Preventing Tragedies Between Police and Communities Act requires a state or local government that receives funding under the JAG program to train law enforcement officers on de-escalation techniques and require law enforcement to use such techniques.

name: Rep. Gerry Connolly
tweet: 5. And his Police Training and Independent Review Act, which authorizes the DOJ to award grants to States that require law enforcement officers to be trained on ethics and racial bias, cultural diversity, and police interaction with the disabled, mentally ill, and new immigrants.

name: Rep. Gerry Connolly
tweet: 4. @RepCohen's National Statistics on Deadly Force Transparency Act, which would require federal, state, and local law enforcement agencies to collect and publish data on the use of deadly force by law enforcement officers.

name: Rep. Gerry Connolly
tweet: 3. @RepJeffries' Eric Garner Excessive Use of Force Prevention Act, which makes the use of chokeholds by law enforcement unlawful through an amendment to civil rights statute.

name: Rep. Gerry Connolly
tweet: 2. @JacksonLeeTX18's End Racial Profiling Act, which prohibits federal, state, and local law enforcement from racial profiling.

name: Rep. Gerry Connolly
tweet: 1. @RepHankJohnson's Stop Militarizing Law Enforcement Act, which would end the transfers of certain military equipment to local law enforcement. In 2017 President Trump fully restored DOD's program that transferred more than $6 billion in military equipment to our streets.
